title: Latent degradation state plus diverse categorical models
summary: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit stops as resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit stops as resets. A compact approximation can create residual degradation states, while CatBoost/stacked AutoGluon can exploit categorical Race/Driver/Compound interactions differently from the current GBM/XGB-only setup.
implementation_hint: Fit per Race-Lap or Race-Compound baseline pace, then build driver-stint residual features: exponentially weighted residual pace, residual slope, residual acceleration, and post-pit reset indicators. Train a CatBoost model with native categorical columns and blend it with the current AutoGluon GBM/XGB; optionally allow AutoGluon bagging/weighted ensemble under a larger time limit.
expected_effect: Adds both new sequential signal and model-family diversity, often useful when top single-tree pipelines plateau near the same CV score.
risk: Sequential residual features are sensitive to row ordering and missing early-stint history; larger ensembles increase runtime and can overfit random validation.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
